title: oxybaphus pseudaggregatus
intro: Oxybaphus pseudaggregatus, also known as Mexican poppy or desert poppy, is a plant species belonging to the Four o'clock family (Nyctaginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Oxybaphus pseudaggregatus are attributed to its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal purposes, or used topically as a poultice or salve for skin issues and wounds.
